steps.title: Keep subject and verb together
steps.content: The name Dr. Ruben G. Mendoza is the subject of the sentence, and studies is the verb that follows. A subject should not be separated from its verb by punctuation unless an interrupting phrase requires it, and no such phrase appears here.

steps.title: Remove the unnecessary mark
steps.content: Choice A uses a semicolon, which separates independent clauses. Choice C inserts a comma between subject and verb, and Choice D uses a colon where no list or explanation follows a complete clause. The grammatical sentence simply continues from the full name to the verb without punctuation. The answer is B.
